_doc: Provenance manifest for vendored Rally Point substrate. Maps each scripts/rally_point/<name>.py to its upstream agent-rally-point counterpart (path relative to the ARP repo root) and the SHA-256 of that UPSTREAM file at last reconcile. The drift-detector (sync_rally.py) recomputes the CURRENT upstream hash and flags DRIFT when upstream moved since this baseline — i.e. 'review whether to port upstream changes into the (intentionally diverged) build-loop copy'. source: null marks a build-loop-original file with no upstream counterpart; the detector skips it cleanly. Read-only; never auto-updates a vendored file. Mirrors scripts/sync_skills.py exactly.

fact_v1.py._doc: Tracks the REAL wire contract the emitter must match: FACT_SCHEMA is defined in the Rust lib.rs (lib.rs:40) and migrate-legacy SILENTLY skips any line whose schema != it (discovery.rs:712-714). Drift here = a future silent-data-loss seam, so the detector watches the Rust source — not a Python mirror — read-only/report-only.

fact_v1_struct._doc: The Fact struct wire shape the emitter targets (store.rs). Additive-tolerant (no deny_unknown_fields) — tracked so a struct-level contract move surfaces for review.

decay.py._doc: Python MIRROR of the canonical Rust recency-decay + size-scaled reclaim policy (decay.rs). The two coordination systems are intentionally SEPARATE codebases; this manifest entry is the COMPATIBILITY guard — drift here means the half-life curve / archive floor / reclaim-timeout math diverged between agent-rally-point and the build-loop fallback. Parity is double-pinned by the byte-identical golden fixture decay_vectors.json (tracked below). Read-only/report-only.

decay_vectors.json._doc: Golden decay vectors. MUST stay byte-identical to the Rust fixture so both test suites assert the same curve at the same reference ages — the cross-repo parity proof. Drift = the two implementations no longer verified against one shared truth.

liveness.py._doc: Python MIRROR of the canonical Rust adaptive-liveness policy (liveness.rs). The two coordination systems are intentionally SEPARATE codebases; this entry is the COMPATIBILITY guard — drift here means the adaptive-cadence window math / 4-signal is_live verdict diverged between agent-rally-point and the build-loop fallback. Parity is double-pinned by the byte-identical golden fixture liveness_vectors.json (tracked below). Read-only/report-only.

liveness_vectors.json._doc: Golden adaptive-liveness vectors. MUST stay byte-identical to the Rust fixture crates/rally-cli/tests/fixtures/liveness_vectors.json so both test suites assert the same window math + is_live verdicts at the same reference cases — the cross-repo parity proof. Drift = the two implementations no longer verified against one shared truth.

heartbeat_parity_vectors.json._doc: Golden heartbeat parity vectors — byte-identical to the Rust fixture crates/rally-cli/tests/fixtures/heartbeat_parity_vectors.json; proves claude/codex heartbeats decay identically (tool-agnostic).
